title: Cleaning Up a Biohazardous Spill Outside of a Biosafety Cabinet
author: Steven W Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wgjfbun

steps.title: Close off the area and allow aerosols to settle
steps.instruction: Close off the area and allow aerosols to settle.

steps.title: Assemble all spill cleanup materials and review procedure
steps.instruction: Assemble all spill cleanup materials and review procedure.

steps.title: Don appropriate PPE
steps.instruction: Don appropriate PPE: laboratory coat, safety glasses or chemical splash goggles (depending on risk of splashes), Nitrile gloves.

steps.title: Pour (don’t spray) disinfectant to contaminated...
steps.instruction: Pour (don’t spray) disinfectant to contaminated surface that is covered with paper towels by pouring it around the periphery of the spill area moving inward. Allow the appropriate contact time for the disinfectant and agent.

steps.title: Wipe up spill
steps.instruction: Wipe up spill. Dispose of the waste in the biohazardous waste containers.

steps.title: Re-apply disinfectant to contaminated surface and...
steps.instruction: Re-apply disinfectant to contaminated surface and allow it to stand for proper contact time.

steps.title: Wipe up disinfectant, repeat if necessary
steps.instruction: Wipe up disinfectant, repeat if necessary.

steps.title: Segregate contaminated cleanup materials into the appropriate biohazardous waste containers
steps.instruction: Segregate contaminated cleanup materials into the appropriate biohazardous waste containers.
